mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal fails to separate positive from negative return days with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of stable inflation indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, or the mechanism shows negative predictive power in low-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the 12-month trailing CPI inflation rate by more than 1.5 percentage points, real borrowing costs compress corporate margins and suppress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in > 50% of periods where real rates exceed 1.5%, or the threshold condition shows no predictive edge over a naive buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm genuine labor market strength, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for prior month market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity that supports corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production coinciding with weakening retail sales signals a synchronized supply-demand contraction that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual decline signal yields positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism fails to outperform a market-neutral benchmark during documented recession periods.
